A minority of runs exhibit prolonged persistence, maintaining small populations of 1 to 5 individuals until approximately step 1500 to 2000, though these eventually decline to zero as well. Given the calibration of 365 ticks per year, this extinction dynamic unfolds over approximately 4 to 5.5 years. of decay suggests discrete mortality events rather than continuous exponential decline. Under the parameter configuration tested—incorporating 20 human foragers, high environmental seasonality (0.8), female food value of 27, and birth spacing of 730 ticks with nursing offspring increasing target value—these dynamics indicate persistent hunting pressure drives female megaherbivore populations toward inevitable demographic collapse despite stochastic variation in extinction timing.Parameter configuration tested—incorporating 20 human foragers, high environmental seasonality (0.8), female food value of 27, female food value of 27, and birth spacing of 730 ticks with nursing offspring increasing target value—these dynamics indicate persistent hunting pressure drives female megaherbivore populations toward inevitable demographic collapse despite stochastic variation in extinction timing.Parameter configuration tested—with 20 human foragers, the total energy of prey animals follows a consistent trajectory of decline from initial values of approximately 25,000 energy units toward effective extinction (near-zero energy), with complete collapse occurring in all observed trajectories.
